_meta.source: Decision 14/2025/QD-TTg (tariff structure, effective 2025-05-29), Decision 599/QD-EVN (avg price, effective 2025-05-10), Decision 1279/QD-BCT (retail pricing by customer group, 2025-05-09)
_meta.notes: Average retail electricity price VND 2,204.0655/kWh excl. VAT as of 2025-05-10 per Decision 599/QD-EVN. Rate multipliers are percentages of the average price per Decision 14/2025/QD-TTg. TOU hours per DFDL analysis of Decision 14. Multipliers expressed as decimal (e.g., 157% = 1.57). EVN can adjust avg price by 2-5% with 3-month minimum interval per Decision 07/2025/QD-TTg (price band VND 1,826.22 - 2,444.09/kWh).
changelog: 2025.2: Corrected all rate multipliers to match Decision 14/2025/QD-TTg table. Fixed voltage tier breakpoints for production tariff (220kV, >35kV-<220kV, >1kV-35kV, ≤1kV). Expanded commercial into 3 subcategories per Decision 14. Corrected household from 6 tiers to 5 tiers. Added two-part tariff trial data per Decree 146/2025/ND-CP. Updated Decree 57 DPPA data.
2025.1: Initial version based on preliminary sources. Contained incorrect multipliers and voltage tier breakpoints.

public_holidays: Hung Kings Commemoration (10th day 3rd lunar month)
tou_schedule.notes: Hours are 0-indexed (0=midnight-1am). Decision 14 specifies: Peak Mon-Sat 9:30-11:30 & 17:00-20:00; Standard Mon-Sat 4:00-9:30, 11:30-17:00, 20:00-22:00; Sunday+holidays 4:00-22:00; Off-peak all days 22:00-4:00. APPROXIMATION: Hourly discretization maps 9:30-11:30 to hours [9,10] and 17:00-20:00 to hours [17,18,19]. Hour 9 (9:00-10:00) is actually ~50% standard / ~50% peak. Hour 11 (11:00-12:00) is ~50% peak / ~50% standard. For higher fidelity, use 30-minute timesteps.

rate_multipliers.notes: Multiplier of base_avg_price_vnd_per_kwh. Values from Decision 14/2025/QD-TTg retail tariff structure table. To compute VND/kWh rate: multiply base_avg_price by multiplier.

production.notes: Category 1 in Decision 14: 'Electricity for Production' (Sản xuất). This is the correct category for manufacturing facilities in industrial parks.

public_services.notes: Category 2 in Decision 14: 'Electricity for Public Services'. Flat rate (no TOU). Subcategories: healthcare/education and public lighting/admin.

commercial.notes: Category 3 in Decision 14: 'Electricity for Commercial Use'. Three subcategories with very different rates. 'Other commercial' is significantly more expensive than production rates.

tourism_accommodation.notes: Category 3.1: Tourism Accommodation Establishments. Rates identical to production at same voltage.

ev_charging.notes: Category 3.2: Electric Vehicle Charging. New category in Decision 14.

other_commercial.notes: Category 3.3: Other Commercial Businesses. Highest TOU rates in the tariff structure.

household.notes: Category 4 in Decision 14: 'Residential Electricity'. Tiered block tariff (not TOU). 5 tiers per Decision 14, replacing the previous 6-tier structure. Multipliers apply to total consumption in each tier bracket.

demand_charge.notes: Vietnam is transitioning to a two-part tariff (capacity + energy) per Electricity Law 2024 Art. 50 and Decree 146/2025/ND-CP. Paper-based pilot Jan-Jun 2026 for large industrial customers (≥200,000 kWh/month avg). Not yet applied to actual bills. Set to 0 for current modeling; use trial values for forward-looking scenarios.

two_part_tariff_trial.notes: Trial capacity charges released by EVN Oct 2025 per Decree 146/2025/ND-CP. Paper trial only (not charged on actual bills during pilot). Energy charges under two-part model differ from single-component rates above. Target: large industrial customers ≥200,000 kWh/month connected at ≥22kV via EVN distribution units.

energy_charge_vnd_per_kwh.notes: Approximate ranges from EVN trial release. Normal/peak/off-peak by voltage level.

decree_57_dppa.notes: Decree 57/2025/ND-CP (effective 2025-03-03) replaced Decree 80/2024/ND-CP after only 8 months. Governs Direct Power Purchase Agreements between RE generators and large electricity consumers. Two models: private wire and grid-connected.

private_wire.notes: Model 1: Direct sale via private connection line (not connected to national grid). No application/registration required, but must report PPA to provincial People's Committee, local power company, and system operator.
private_wire.price_cap: Negotiated price cannot exceed MOIT ceiling generation tariff for the RE type
private_wire.rooftop_solar_surplus_price: Average market electricity price from prior year, capped at highest ground-mounted solar ceiling tariff

grid_connected.notes: Model 2: Via national grid through forward contracts. Three contractual relationships: (1) generator-consumer forward contract, (2) generator-EVN spot market PPA, (3) consumer-EVN retail PPA. Generator must own RE plant ≥10 MW.

customer_eligibility.notes: Decree 57 replaced Decree 80's fixed 200,000 kWh/month threshold with a flexible 'Minimum Take Amount' set by MOIT per wholesale market rules. Expanded from production-only to include service providers and EV charging operators.
customer_eligibility.minimum_consumption: Per MOIT wholesale market regulations (dynamic, no longer fixed at 200,000 kWh/month)
customer_eligibility.voltage_requirement: Per MOIT regulations (no longer fixed at ≥22kV)
customer_eligibility.ongoing_compliance: Large customers in DPPA for ≥12 months must purchase from EVN the Minimum Take Amount for the 12-month period ending Oct 31 of previous calendar year

solar_ceiling_tariffs_vnd_per_kwh.notes: Maximum generation tariffs for private wire DPPAs set by MOIT per Decree 57. Vary by technology, storage, and region (North/Central/South). Excl. VAT.

bess_incentive_thresholds.notes: BESS paired with solar qualifies for higher ceiling tariffs if it meets all three thresholds below.
